title: How to use opencis
text: can install released version opencis CRAN: can install development version GitHub using remotes package:

what: Searching for studies, questions and series
title: How to use opencis
text: search_cis() searches CIS catalogue returns tibble matching results. catalogo argument controls type item searched: "estudio" (default), "pregunta" "serie". can restrict results date range , change sort order sort ("relevance", "publishDate-", "publishDate+"). default search_cis() returns first page results. Use search_all_cis() automatically paginate pages get every matching result single tibble: search_all_cis() accepts arguments search_cis().
code: library(opencis)  # Search for survey studies search_cis(q = "preelectoral", from = "2020-01-01", to = "2023-11-17")  # Search for survey questions search_cis(q = "feminismo", catalogo = "pregunta")  # Search for data series search_cis(q = "situación económica", catalogo = "serie") # Retrieve all postelectoral studies (all pages) all_studies <- search_all_cis(q = "postelectoral") print(nrow(all_studies))  # Filter by date range across all pages studies <- search_all_cis(   q    = "ideologia",   from = "2010-01-01",   to   = "2020-12-31" )

title: How to use opencis
text: read_cis() downloads SPSS data file study imports directly R labelled data frame (via haven):

title: How to use opencis
text: loading study read_cis(), use get_data_dictionary() obtain tidy tibble every variable name, label value labels:
code: df   <- read_cis(3328) dict <- get_data_dictionary(df) print(dict)  # Find variables whose label contains a keyword dict[grepl("sexo", dict$label, ignore.case = TRUE), ]  # Inspect value labels for a specific variable dict$value_labels[[which(dict$variable == "SEXO")]]

title: How to use opencis
text: get_metadata() retrieves technical information sheet study CIS website — field dates, study type, country, authorship, thematic indices, etc. — returns two-column tibble (field, value):

what: Downloading the ZIP file to disk
title: How to use opencis
text: want keep raw data files instead reading temporary directory, use download_study(). saves ZIP archive local folder:
code: # Save to the current working directory path <- download_study(3328) cat("Saved to:", path, "\n")  # Save to a specific folder path <- download_study(3328, destdir = "data/raw") cat("Saved to:", path, "\n")

what: Browsing the questionnaire and technical sheet
title: How to use opencis
text: browse_pdf() extracts PDF documents bundled inside study ZIP opens default browser. CIS ZIPs typically include two PDFs: Questionnaire (wanted_file = "cues", default) Technical sheet (wanted_file = "ft")
code: # Open the questionnaire for study 3328 browse_pdf(3328)  # Open the technical sheet browse_pdf(3328, wanted_file = "ft")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Héctor Meleiro. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Meleiro H (2026). opencis: Import Data Spanish Sociological Research Center (CIS). R package version 0.1.0, https://opencis.spainelectoralproject.com.
code: @Manual{,   title = {opencis: Import Data from Spanish Sociological Research Center (CIS)},   author = {Héctor Meleiro},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://opencis.spainelectoralproject.com}, }

what: Import Data from Spanish Sociological Research Center (CIS)
title: Import Data from Spanish Sociological Research Center (CIS)
text: Search import data directly R Spanish Sociological Research Center (CIS). CIS public institution conducts electoral sociological research studies Spanish society. CIS large database surveys can accessed website. package includes functions search surveys, survey questions timeseries, import data directly R.

title: Import Data from Spanish Sociological Research Center (CIS)
text: can install released version opencis CRAN: can install development version GitHub using remotes package:

title: Import Data from Spanish Sociological Research Center (CIS)
text: package includes two functions. first function used search surveys, survey questions data series second function used import data directly R.
code: # Search for survey studies library(opencis)  # Search for survey studies search_cis(q = "preelectoral", from = "2020-01-01", to = "2023-11-17")  #> # A tibble: 27 × 4 #>    study   date       title                                                              url   #>    <chr>   <date>     <chr>                                                              <chr> #>  1 3411    2023-06-08 Preelectoral elecciones generales 2023                             http… #>  2 3402    2023-04-10 Preelectoral elecciones municipales y autonómicas 2023             http… #>  3 3306    2021-01-02 Preelectoral de Cataluña. Elecciones autonómicas 2021              http… #>  4 3317    2021-03-19 Preelectoral elecciones autonómicas 2021. Comunidad de Madrid      http… #>  5 3287    2020-06-10 Preelectoral de Galicia. Elecciones autonómicas julio 2020         http… #>  6 3276    2020-02-17 Preelectoral de Galicia. Elecciones autonómicas abril 2020         http… #>  7 3286    2020-06-10 Preelectoral del País Vasco. Elecciones autonómicas julio 2020     http… #>  8 3275    2020-02-17 Preelectoral del País Vasco. Elecciones autonómicas abril 2020     http… #>  9 3365    2022-05-17 Preelectoral elecciones autonómicas 2022. Comunidad autónoma de A… http… #> 10 3348    2022-01-07 Preelectoral elecciones autonómicas 2022. Comunidad autónoma de C… http… #> # ℹ 17 more rows #> # ℹ Use `print(n = ...)` to see more rows   # Search for survey questions search_cis(q = "feminismo", catalogo = "pregunta")  #> # A tibble: 8 × 4 #>   question             date       title                                                  url   #>   <chr>                <date>     <chr>                                                  <chr> #> 1 Pregunta 3428/0/0016 2023-11-06 Escala de autodefinición feminista (0-10)              http… #> 2 Pregunta 2212/0/0008 1996-04-13 Sentimientos hacia diferentes ideologías, situaciones… http… #> 3 Pregunta 2828/0/0010 2010-01-09 Rasgos ideológicos que definen a la derecha y a la iz… http… #> 4 Pregunta 1993/0/0009 1992-02-27 Sentimientos hacia diferentes ideologías, situaciones… http… #> 5 Pregunta 2401/0/0025 2000-12-09 Rasgos ideológicos que definen a la derecha y a la iz… http… #> 6 Pregunta 3428/0/0010 2023-11-06 Aprobación de diferentes conductas pro-igualdad de gé… http… #> 7 Pregunta 2016/0/0026 1992-07-09 Rasgos que definen a la derecha y a la izquierda polí… http… #> 8 Pregunta 2154/0/0036 1995-04-03 Rasgos que definen a la derecha y a la izquierda polí… http…  # Search for data series search_cis(q = "situación económica", catalogo = "serie")  #> # A tibble: 100 × 5 #>    serie               from       to         title                                                               url     #>    <chr>               <date>     <date>     <chr>                                                               <chr>   #>  1 Serie K.1.03.03.012 2011-11-21 2025-09-12 Situación económica familiar (ICC)                                  https:… #>  2 Serie K.1.02.01.014 1992-11-13 2019-10-01 Valoración de la situación económica actual de España (Extremadura) https:… #>  3 Serie K.1.02.01.008 1992-11-14 2019-06-17 Valoración de la situación económica actual de España (Cantabria)   https:… #>  4 Serie K.1.02.01.003 1986-05-01 2019-10-01 Valoración de la situación económica actual de España (Andalucía)   https:… #>  5 Serie K.1.02.01.004 1998-05-01 2019-10-01 Valoración de la situación económica actual de España (Aragón)      https:… #>  6 Serie K.1.02.01.007 1998-05-01 2019-10-01 Valoración de la situación económica actual de España (Canarias)    https:… #>  7 Serie K.1.02.01.019 1998-05-01 2015-05-29 Valoración de la situación económica actual de España (La Rioja)    https:… #>  8 Serie K.1.02.01.011 1992-11-07 2020-03-01 Valoración de la situación económica actual de España (Cataluña)    https:… #>  9 Serie K.1.03.01.012 2010-01-23 2017-09-21 Valoración de la situación económica personal actual (Andalucía)    https:… #> 10 Serie K.1.03.01.020 1992-04-24 2025-10-01 Valoración de la situación económica personal actual (Nacional)     https:… #> # what: Open the questionnaire PDF of a CIS study — browse_pdf
title: Open the questionnaire PDF of a CIS study — browse_pdf
text: Opens PDF document CIS study default browser.

title: Open the questionnaire PDF of a CIS study — browse_pdf
Open the questionnaire PDF of a CIS study — browse_pdf
text: study_code string study code. wanted_file keyword used match PDF filename inside ZIP. Use "cues" (default) questionnaire "ft" technical sheet.

title: Open the questionnaire PDF of a CIS study — browse_pdf
text: Called side effect opening PDF browser. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Open the questionnaire PDF of a CIS study — browse_pdf
text: CIS study ZIP files typically contain two PDF documents: questionnaire (cuestionario): use wanted_file = "cues". technical sheet (ficha técnica): use wanted_file = "ft".

title: Open the questionnaire PDF of a CIS study — browse_pdf
code: if (interactive()) { # Open the questionnaire (cuestionario) for study 3328 browse_pdf("3328")  # Open the technical sheet (ficha técnica) for study 3328 browse_pdf("3328", wanted_file = "ft") }

title: Build CIS catalog URL with date range — cis_catalog_url_date
text: start Integer. starting page search results. Default 1, iterate get results. q String. search query. Default empty string. Date NULL. start date filtering results. Default NULL Date NULL. end date filtering results. Default NULL. sort String. sorting order results ("publishDate-", "publishDate+", "relevance"). Default "relevance". catalogo String. catalog type ("estudio", "pregunta", "serie"). Default "estudio". ... Additional parameters (used).

title: Build CIS catalog URL with date range — cis_catalog_url_date
text: string representing constructed URL.

title: Find CIS study data URL in HTML content — find_url
Find CIS study data URL in HTML content — find_url
text: html character string containing HTML content search. ids optional vector two strings representing numeric subroutes URL (e.g., c("3411", "3411") "https://www.cis.es/documents/3411/3411/MD3411.zip"). NULL, function searches valid CIS study data URL. allow_uuid boolean indicating whether allow UUIDs URL. Defaults FALSE.

title: Find CIS study data URL in HTML content — find_url
text: character vector unique URLs found HTML content.

what: Extract a data dictionary from a CIS study data frame — get_data_dictionary
title: Extract a data dictionary from a CIS study data frame — get_data_dictionary
text: Returns tibble listing variable data along variable label value labels, loaded haven.

title: Extract a data dictionary from a CIS study data frame — get_data_dictionary
Extract a data dictionary from a CIS study data frame — get_data_dictionary
text: data data.frame loaded CIS .sav file, typically output read_cis.

title: Extract a data dictionary from a CIS study data frame — get_data_dictionary
text: tibble columns: variable Variable name. label Variable label, NA none. value_labels named numeric vector value labels, NULL unlabelled variables (list-column).

title: Extract a data dictionary from a CIS study data frame — get_data_dictionary
code: # Create a small labelled data frame df <- data.frame(   SEXO = haven::labelled(c(1, 2, 1), labels = c(Hombre = 1, Mujer = 2)),   EDAD = c(34, 51, 29) ) attr(df$SEXO, "label") <- "Sexo" attr(df$EDAD, "label") <- "Edad"  # Inspect its variable dictionary dict <- get_data_dictionary(df) print(dict) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   variable label value_labels #>   <chr>    <chr> <named list> #> 1 SEXO     Sexo  <dbl [2]>    #> 2 EDAD     Edad  <NULL>        # Find variables with a specific keyword in their label dict[grepl("sexo", dict$label, ignore.case = TRUE), ] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   variable label value_labels #>   <chr>    <chr> <named list> #> 1 SEXO     Sexo  <dbl [2]>     # Inspect value labels for a specific variable sex_var <- match("SEXO", dict$variable) if (!is.na(sex_var)) {   dict$value_labels[[sex_var]] } #> Hombre  Mujer  #>      1      2

what: opencis: Import Data from Spanish Sociological Research Center (CIS) — opencis-package

text: Search import data directly R Spanish Sociological Research Center (CIS) https://www.cis.es/inicio. CIS public institution conducts electoral sociological research studies Spanish society. CIS large database surveys can accessed website. package includes functions search surveys, survey questions timeseries, import data directly R.

ℹ 3,196 more rows #> # ℹ 226 more variables: SEXO <dbl+lbl>, EDAD <dbl+lbl>, P0 <dbl+lbl>, #> #   P1 <dbl+lbl>, P1A <dbl+lbl>, P2 <dbl+lbl>, P3 <dbl+lbl>, P4 <dbl+lbl>, #> #   P5_1 <dbl+lbl>, P5_2 <dbl+lbl>, P5_3 <dbl+lbl>, P5_4 <dbl+lbl>, #> #   P6 <dbl+lbl>, P6A <dbl+lbl>, P6AR <dbl+lbl>, P6B <dbl+lbl>, P7_1 <dbl+lbl>, #> #   P7_2 <dbl+lbl>, P7_3 <dbl+lbl>, P7IMPRESA <dbl+lbl>, P7DIGITAL <dbl+lbl>, #> #   P7TV <dbl+lbl>, P7RADIO <dbl+lbl>, P7A <dbl+lbl>, P7B <dbl+lbl>, …  # code: search_all_cis(   q = "",   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   sort = "relevance",   catalogo = "estudio",   ... )

title: Search all CIS results with automatic pagination — search_all_cis
text: q String. search query. Default empty string. Date NULL. start date filtering results. Default NULL. date format must "YYYY-MM-DD". Date NULL. end date filtering results. Default NULL. date format must "YYYY-MM-DD". sort String. sorting order results ("publishDate-", "publishDate+", "relevance"). Default "relevance". catalogo String. catalog type ("estudio", "pregunta", "serie"). Default "estudio". ... Additional parameters passed search_cis.

title: Search all CIS results with automatic pagination — search_all_cis
text: tibble search results across pages.

title: Search all CIS results with automatic pagination — search_all_cis
code: # \donttest{ # Retrieve all postelectoral studies (all pages) all_studies <- search_all_cis(q = "postelectoral") print(nrow(all_studies)) #> [1] 191  # Filter by date range studies_2010_2020 <- search_all_cis(   q    = "ideologia",   from = "2010-01-01",   to   = "2020-12-31" ) print(studies_2010_2020) #> # A tibble: 163 × 4 #>    study date       title                                                  url   #>    <chr> <date>     <chr>                                                  <chr> #>  1 2930  2012-01-20 Congruencia ideológica entre electores y representant… http… #>  2 3257  2019-07-01 Barómetro de julio 2019                                http… #>  3 3082  2015-05-01 Barómetro de mayo 2015                                 http… #>  4 2938  2012-03-21 Actitudes de la juventud en España hacia el emprendim… http… #>  5 3150  2016-09-12 Percepción de la discriminación en España (II)         http… #>  6 2847  2010-10-04 Barómetro de octubre 2010                              http… #>  7 2967  2012-10-31 Actitudes hacia la inmigración (VI)                    http… #>  8 3119  2015-11-19 Actitudes hacia la inmigración (VIII)                  http… #>  9 2918  2011-11-05 Actitudes hacia la inmigración (V)                     http… #> 10 2846  2010-09-21 Actitudes hacia la inmigración (IV)                    http… #> # ℹ 153 more rows # }

title: Search for CIS studies. — search_cis
code: search_cis(   start = 1,   q = "",   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   sort = "relevance",   catalogo = "estudio",   ... )

title: Search for CIS studies. — search_cis
text: start Integer. starting page search results. Default 1, iterate get results. q String. search query. Default empty string. Date NULL. start date filtering results. Default NULL. date format must "YYYY-MM-DD". Date NULL. end date filtering results. Default NULL. date format must "YYYY-MM-DD". sort String. sorting order results ("publishDate-", "publishDate+", "relevance"). Default "relevance". catalogo String. catalog type ("estudio", "pregunta", "serie"). Default "estudio". ... Additional parameters (used).

title: Search for CIS studies. — search_cis
text: data.frame search results.

title: Search for CIS studies. — search_cis
code: # \donttest{ # Search by search terms studies <- search_cis(q = "postelectoral") print(studies) #> # A tibble: 50 × 4 #>    study date       title                                   url                  #>    <chr> <date>     <chr>                                   <chr>                #>  1 1497  1985-12-01 Postelectoral Galicia 1985              https://www.cis.es/… #>  2 1292  1981-11-03 Postelectoral Galicia 1981              https://www.cis.es/… #>  3 2199  1995-11-28 Postelectoral Cataluña 1995             https://www.cis.es/… #>  4 1998  1992-03-25 Postelectoral Cataluña 1992             https://www.cis.es/… #>  5 1750  1988-06-02 Postelectoral Cataluña 1988             https://www.cis.es/… #>  6 1903  1990-11-08 Postelectoral País Vasco 1990           https://www.cis.es/… #>  7 1402  1984-03-01 Postelectoral País Vasco 1984           https://www.cis.es/… #>  8 2120  1994-10-28 Postelectoral País Vasco 1994           https://www.cis.es/… #>  9 1565  1986-12-01 Postelectoral País Vasco 1986           https://www.cis.es/… #> 10 1327  1982-11-01 Postelectoral elecciones generales 1982 https://www.cis.es/… #> # ℹ 40 more rows  # Narrow the search by dates studies <- search_cis(q = "postelectoral",                           from = "2011-01-01",                           to = "2020-01-01") print(studies) #> # A tibble: 50 × 4 #>    study date       title                                                  url   #>    <chr> <date>     <chr>                                                  <chr> #>  1 3248  2019-05-10 Postelectoral elecciones generales 2019                http… #>  2 3145  2016-07-04 Postelectoral elecciones generales 2016                http… #>  3 2890  2011-05-27 Postelectoral elecciones municipales 2011. Barcelona   http… #>  4 3085  2015-05-31 Postelectoral elecciones municipales 2015. Barcelona   http… #>  5 3155  2016-09-28 Postelectoral de Galicia. Elecciones autonómicas 2016  http… #>  6 3202  2017-12-28 Postelectoral de Cataluña. Elecciones autonómicas 2017 http… #>  7 3113  2015-09-30 Postelectoral de Cataluña. Elecciones autonómicas 2015 http… #>  8 3028  2014-05-29 Postelectoral elecciones al Parlamento Europeo 2014    http… #>  9 2963  2012-10-25 Postelectoral de Galicia. Elecciones autonómicas 2012  http… #> 10 3253  2019-06-17 Postelectoral elecciones autonómicas y municipales 20… http… #> # ℹ 40 more rows  # Use the catalogo parameter to search for questions ("pregunta") or data series ("serie") studies <- search_cis(q = "ideologia",                           from = "2011-01-01",                           to = "2020-01-01",                           catalogo = "serie") print(studies) #> # A tibble: 0 × 2 #> # ℹ 2 variables: title <chr>, url <chr> # }
